text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报道。

text: In December, doctors in Mumbai, India reported about a group of patients
zh: 去年12月，印度孟买的医生报告了一组患者的病例，

text: with what they called totally drug -resistant tuberculosis.
zh: 他们将其称为“完全耐药性肺结核”。

text: Indian health officials have been investigating these cases.
zh: 印度卫生官员一直在调查这些病例。

text: But there have been reports of untreatable cases of TB in the past.
zh: 但过去也曾有过无法治愈的肺结核病例报告。

text: Doctors reported 15 patients in Iran
zh: 医生曾报告伊朗有15名患者，

text: in 2009 and 2 patients in Italy in 2007.
zh: 发生在2009年，意大利在2007年也有2名患者。

text: Tuberculosis is a bacterial disease that usually targets the lungs.
zh: 肺结核是一种通常侵袭肺部的细菌性疾病。

text: It causes an estimated 5 ,000 deaths each day
zh: 据估计，它每天造成约5000人死亡，

text: or about 2 million a year. Some forms of TB bacteria
zh: 每年约200万人死亡。某些类型的结核病菌

text: no longer react to one or more of the antibiotics commonly used to cure the disease.
zh: 已不再对一种或多种常用于治疗该病的抗生素产生反应。

text: These are known as drug -resistant strains.
zh: 这些被称为耐药菌株。

text: Some resist even more drugs. The World Health Organization
zh: 有些甚至对更多药物产生抗药性。世界卫生组织

text: says 69 countries have reported cases of extensively drug -resistant tuberculosis.
zh: 表示，已有69个国家报告了广泛耐药性肺结核病例。

text: The WHO says at least 25 ,000 such cases are reported worldwide every year.
zh: 世卫组织称，全球每年至少报告25000例此类病例。

text: The agency's director general, Dr. Margaret Chan, is concerned about the spread of drug -resistant
zh: 该机构总干事陈冯富珍博士对耐药结核病的蔓延表示担忧。

text: She says, call it what you may, a time mom or a powder keg,
zh: 她说，无论你称它为定时炸弹还是火药桶，

text: any way you look at it, this is a potentially explosive situation.
zh: 无论怎么看，这都是一种潜在的爆炸性局势。

text: Officials say drug -resistant TB has been a growing problem in countries like India and China.
zh: 官员们表示，耐药性结核病在印度和中国等国家正日益成为一个严重问题。

text: In many cases, doctors misdiagnose patients
zh: 在许多情况下，医生对患者的诊断有误，

text: or give them the wrong treatment or not enough treatment. Misuse of these antibiotics
zh: 或给予错误的治疗，或治疗不足。滥用这些抗生素

text: increases the danger that the bacteria will develop resistance to the drugs.
zh: 增加了细菌对药物产生耐药性的风险。

text: Neeraj Mistry is a public health doctor.
zh: 尼拉杰·米斯特里是一位公共卫生医生。

text: He says surveys show that very few Indian doctors are treating TB patients
zh: 他说，调查显示，很少有印度医生能

text: with the right drugs for the right length of time.
zh: 以正确的药物和正确的疗程来治疗结核病患者。

text: Another problem is that patients may not take all of their medicine.
zh: 另一个问题是，患者可能没有按规定服完全部药物。

text: The emergence of totally resistant TB is a result of failed public health intervention strategies.
zh: 完全耐药性结核病的出现，是公共卫生干预策略失败的结果。

text: When we deliver ineffective treatment regimens
zh: 当我们采用无效的治疗方案时，

text: and when we don't have full adherence and compliance to treatment,
zh: 以及当患者未能完全遵从治疗时，

text: it enables the emergence of resistance within the individual. The WHO
zh: 这就使得耐药性得以在个体体内产生。世卫组织

text: says new, stronger TB drugs could be available by the end of this year or early 2013.
zh: 表示，更强效的新型结核病药物有望在今年年底或2013年初问世。

text: Researchers are also working on vaccines to prevent the disease.
zh: 研究人员也在致力于研发预防该病的疫苗。

text: Dr. Ann Ginzburg works at the ARIS Global TB Vaccine Foundation.
zh: 安·金兹伯格博士就职于国际结核病疫苗基金会（Aeras）。

text: She says the development process takes a long time,
zh: 她说，疫苗研发过程需要很长时间，

text: partly because people often do not get sick for years after getting infected with TB. be.
zh: 部分原因是许多人感染结核病后，往往多年都不会发病。

text: So when you do a vaccine trial, you have to vaccinate people and watch them for years.
zh: 因此在进行疫苗试验时，必须给人们接种疫苗后，再观察他们数年之久。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Carolyn Presutti.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语节目，我是卡罗琳·普雷苏蒂。
